content:
- All required gates are now green: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- Marked the active docs task completed with passes=true and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh; Ralph updated the task lists and advanced current_task.txt.
- Next I am committing every changed file, including the Ralph bookkeeping files, with the required task-finished commit message and then pushing.

- Committed everything with the required task-finished message after the post-commit docs publish hook ran successfully.
- git push to the main repo succeeded, and the docs publish hook also pushed the synced mdBook output to the docs repository.
- Work should now be complete; I am only checking for a clean worktree before stopping.

- All required gates passed: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh; it updated current_tasks/current_tasks_done and sent the finish email, then exited non-zero because .ralph/current_task.txt no longer existed after the switch.
- Next I am committing the full task result and pushing, treating the missing current_task.txt as the expected post-switch state rather than a failed completion.

- Final state is clean and already recorded in git: the docs task commit plus follow-up Ralph bookkeeping commits are present, and git push reported Everything up-to-date.
- Verified required gates were green before finalization: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- Stopping now with the task switched away and the repo clean.

- All required gates are green in this turn: make docs-lint, make docs-build, make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed.
- The docs/task implementation itself had already been committed on HEAD as 6c2f0fe, so the only remaining work after my rerun was to re-run task_switch and capture the resulting Ralph bookkeeping deltas.
- Next I am committing the final Ralph state updates with a task-finished message and pushing them so the repo matches the requested completion workflow exactly.

- Re-ran the full gate sequence in the live workspace and confirmed everything is green again: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- The only remaining uncommitted changes are Ralph bookkeeping updates caused by continuing work after the earlier task-finished commit and task switch.
- Next I am committing those remaining .ralph state files and pushing so the repository is clean before stopping.

- Read the progress log and active/done Ralph task summaries for the Choose-Task role.
- Verified there are no active failing bug tasks; only unblocked failing non-bug tasks remain, plus a low-priority hygiene task and a meta-task.
- Opened the candidate task files and checked for blocker tags and priorities. The docs usefulness chain is unblocked; the project-wide magic-number audit is explicitly low priority. Chose the contributor-docs rebuild task as the best next step because it establishes the docs direction, task 04 explicitly belongs at the end of that story, and nothing higher priority is available.
- Next action is to write `.ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/02-task-rebuild-contributor-docs-as-codebase-navigation-and-contract-guide.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop immediately.

- Read the Ralph progress log, current task pointer, and the active contributor-doc task file.
- Inspected the current contributor docs plus high-signal repo entrypoints (`docs/src/contributors/*`, `docs/src/SUMMARY.md`, `src/lib.rs`, `src/bin/pgtuskmaster.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`) to ground a real plan instead of writing a generic one.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/02-task-rebuild-contributor-docs-as-codebase-navigation-and-contract-guide.md` and ended it with `TO BE VERIFIED` per the workflow. I noticed the contributor docs are already fairly strong, so the skeptical pass needs to challenge whether the existing page split should be preserved or reorganized before any rewrite starts.
- Next step, if resuming immediately, is to read the task file again, perform the required deep skeptical review, change at least one concrete part of the plan, replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE`, and then stop again unless explicitly continuing into execution.

- Read the prior Ralph log and current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/02-task-rebuild-contributor-docs-as-codebase-navigation-and-contract-guide.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether this turn is the skeptical review pass or the execution pass.

- The active task file is currently at TO BE VERIFIED.
- I am doing the required skeptical review now, focusing on whether the planned doc structure actually matches contributor jobs and whether the audit source set is complete enough.
- Next I will amend the plan in the task file with at least one concrete structural change, then replace the marker with NOW EXECUTE.

- The skeptical review found the current plan was too biased toward preserving the existing chapter split.
- It also underspecified startup/bootstrap source files even though contributor navigation depends heavily on runtime bootstrap and initial DCS planning paths.
- I am patching the task file to force evaluation of a more task-oriented chapter split and to expand the required audit evidence before setting NOW EXECUTE.

- Before patching the plan, I am confirming the actual module layout so the skeptical-review changes name real files rather than imagined ones.
- The main review outcome so far is that the plan must force an explicit decision between keeping the current chapter split and consolidating some maintenance-only pages if they dilute navigation value.
- I also expect to add runtime startup/bootstrap files to the mandatory audit list if they exist as separate modules.

- Completed the required TO BE VERIFIED pass on the contributor-docs task.
- I changed the plan concretely: expanded the mandatory source audit set, forced an explicit chapter-split decision instead of assuming the current split is fine, and allowed consolidation of verification/docs-style or testing/harness chapters when that improves contributor navigation.
- Replaced the terminal marker with NOW EXECUTE and stopped without beginning execution, so the next pass can follow the plan exactly as instructed.

- Read the Ralph progress log and current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task file is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/02-task-rebuild-contributor-docs-as-codebase-navigation-and-contract-guide.md.
- Next I am reading that task file to see whether this turn is an execution pass or whether another plan-state transition is still required.

- The active task file already says NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement rather than do another planning-only pass.
- I am doing the focused execution audit now: current contributor docs first, then the named source files needed to ground any rewritten design-contract claims.
- Before rewriting prose I still need to write the concrete mismatch list and the chapter-split decision back into the task file because the plan explicitly requires that evidence.

- I am auditing the testing-system and harness chapters against the actual harness modules and the focused HA e2e scenarios.
- The likely rewrite targets are the boundary between fast tests and ultra-long HA coverage, the observer/invariant guidance, and the exact harness ownership boundaries around runtime config, real-binary provenance, and fault injection.
- Next I will patch only testing-system.md and harness-internals.md with code-grounded navigation and safer change guidance.

- I finished the first pass over the current contributor docs and identified likely drift around module naming, chapter granularity, and some runtime/control-flow wording.
- Now I am validating the exact runtime/state/channel details in the live source so the mismatch list in the task file is evidence-based.
- After that I will patch the task file with the mismatch list and chapter decision, then rewrite docs from those notes instead of improvising.

- The focused audit is turning up a mix of structural and factual drift rather than a blank-slate docs problem.
- I have enough context to parallelize the rewrite, so three bounded workers are now handling runtime/wiring, HA plus API/debug, and testing/harness pages while I own the task-file evidence, chapter decision, and final integration.
- I still need to pin down concrete mismatches in the task file before accepting any prose rewrites, especially stale file references and whether verification/docs-style should remain separate chapters.

- I finished the focused doc audit pass across the contributor pages plus the named runtime/HA/API/harness source files.
- The strongest structural change is to consolidate verification.md and docs-style.md into one contributor docs-maintenance chapter; the harness chapter still earns its own page under testing.
- Next I am writing the concrete mismatch list and chapter decision into the active task file before I start patching contributor docs.

- Patched the active task file with the required execution-time evidence: a concrete mismatch list and a chapter-split decision.
- The main structural choice is to keep testing and harness chapters separate but first-class, while merging verification and docs-style into one contributor-maintenance chapter at docs/src/contributors/verification.md and removing docs-style.md.
- Next I am integrating the parallel doc rewrites, then I will update SUMMARY/navigation and run the full gate sequence including make test-long.

- Rewrote testing-system.md and harness-internals.md to make the live harness call path, nextest gate split, and observer-based HA evidence model explicit.
- The biggest factual corrections were that make test-long is defined by .config/nextest.toml plus Docker smoke steps, and that top-level HA test files are only thin entrypoints while the real scenario logic and invariant proofs live under tests/ha/support/.
- Next I am doing a quick diff/self-check and then I will report the exact files changed and corrections back to the parent agent.

- The testing/harness worker completed and updated those two pages with more concrete start-cluster, observer, nextest-profile, and harness-ownership guidance.
- I am now waiting on the runtime/wiring and HA/API workers while reviewing the already-landed diff plus my own verification/SUMMARY changes for link and structure consistency.
- After the remaining workers return, I need one integration sweep, then docs lint/build and the required full repo gates.

- Recorded the execution mismatch list and chapter split decision in the task file.
- Rewrote the contributor entry/navigation pages (`index`, `codebase-map`, `worker-wiring`) and consolidated the docs-maintenance material into the verification chapter while removing docs-style from navigation.
- Next I am sweeping for stale links/titles and deciding whether the remaining deep-dive pages need focused factual edits before I run the required gates.

- Contributor docs and navigation now build cleanly: make docs-lint and make docs-build both passed.
- The biggest factual fixes were around HA dispatch semantics, API/debug projection ownership, test-harness entrypoints, and consolidating docs maintenance into one chapter.
- Next I am running the required repository gates in full: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.

- Rewrote the contributor orientation, codebase map, worker wiring, HA pipeline, and API/debug contract pages to line up with the current runtime and contract boundaries.
- Integrated the concurrent docs-maintenance merge by keeping the merged maintenance chapter at docs/src/contributors/verification.md and removing the stale docs-style.md page.
- Next I am doing a last stale-link scan, then I will run the full required gates and only mark acceptance once they are green.

- All required gates are green now: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- Before task switch I still need to patch the task file so its status, pass markers, and chapter-merge notes match what actually shipped.
- After that I will run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit every changed file including .ralph state, and push.
